title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 38 38

questions.question: Arrange the following in a logical and meaningful order. (1) Respond (2) Failure (3) Pacific (4) Musical
questions.explanation: Here, the logical and meaningful order according to the alphabetical order is Failure, Musical, Pacific, Respond.

questions.question: Select the odd number from the given options. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 - Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed)
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: Option (a) 784: 282 = 784 Option (b) 528: It is not a perfect square root. Option (c) 1089: 332 = 1089 Option (d) 1156: 342 = 1156

questions.question: Consider the given statements to be true and decide which of the conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statement: As cities grow and consumption patterns change, the volume of solid waste generated escalates, posing significant challenges to sustainable development. Among the traditional methods of waste disposal, burning or incineration has been practised for decades. Conclusions: 1) Improper disposal of solid waste can result in environmental pollution, health risks, and aesthetic degradation. 2) Burning waste may be appropriate if there is no better alternative way to dispose of your waste or if the waste can be used as an efficient fuel.
questions.explanation: Improper disposal of solid waste can result in environmental pollution, health risks, and aesthetic degradation. As there is no information about this in the statement. So, conclusion I is false. Burning waste may be appropriate if there is no better alternative way to dispose of your waste or if the waste can be used as an efficient fuel. As there is no information about this in the statement. So, conclusion II is false. Hence, neither conclusion I nor II follows.

questions.question: Statement: It has been found that the body exhibits similar responses to yoga and stress. This proves that stress is as beneficial for the body as yoga is. Assumption I: Bodily responses are an accurate measure of how beneficial an activity is for the body. Assumption II: Yoga is the most beneficial physical activity for the body.
options: Both assumptions I and II are correct
Neither assumption I nor II is correct
questions.explanation: I needs to be correct as the statement gives the conclusion that stress is as beneficial for the body as yoga, and this would only be true, if I is true. II is incorrect, as the statement gives no info about which activities are beneficial for the body and where yoga ranks among them. option a is the right answer.

questions.question: In the following Venn diagram circle represents those who have travelled to Canada, rectangle represents those who have travelled to Japan and pentagon represents those who have travelled to Russia. What is the number of those who have travelled to exactly two countries ?
questions.explanation: The number of those who have travelled to exactly two countries is = 82 + 46 + 89 = 217.

questions.question: Select the correct sequence of mathematical signs that can sequentially replace the (*) signs and balance the given equation. 25 * 5 * 10 * 5 * 35 * 20
questions.explanation: From option a, we get: 25 + 5 - 10 ÷ 5 x 35 = 20 30 - 70 = 20 So this option is not valid From option b, we get: 25 ÷ 5 + 10 x 5 - 35 = 20 5 + 50 - 35 = 20 20 = 20 LHS = RHS From option c, we get: 25 + 5 - 10 x 5 ÷ 35 = 20 30 - 50 ÷ 35 = 20 So this option is not valid. From option d, we get: 25 ÷ 5 - 10 x 5 + 35 = 20 5 - 50 + 35 = 20 So this option is not valid.

questions.question: Select the figure from the options that can replace the question mark (?) and complete the pattern.
questions.explanation: Thus, option figure (I) will complete the figure.

questions.question: Choose the correct analogous word pair from the given alternatives. Sanskrit : Language
questions.explanation: Sanskrit is a language. Similarly, Shehnai is a musical instrument.

questions.question: Which of the following letter-clusters should replace # and % so that the pattern followed between the left pair is the same as that on the right side of ::? # : TYO :: ABC : %
questions.explanation: Rule: +4, +7, +3 P+4 = T; R+7 = Y; L+3 = O => TYO Similarly A+4 = E; B+7 = I; C+3 = F => EIF

questions.question: Seven words are given out of which six are alike in some manner and one is different. Select the odd word. Beaker, Test tube, Dropper, Pipette, Nitrogen, Funnel, Flask
questions.explanation: All the above are equipment used in chemistry labs except nitrogen which is not equipment but a gas.

questions.question: If the first six letters of the English alphabetical order are written in reverse order and then after A, the next fourteen letters are written in normal order and then are followed by six remaining letters which are written the reverse order. How many letters are between Y and N ?
questions.explanation: From the directions, we get, F E D C B A G H I J K L M N O P Q R S T Z Y X W V U

questions.question: Study the following information and answer the question given below: 'Y ! Z' means 'Z is the sister of Y' 'Y + Z' means 'Z is the daughter of Y's son' 'Y % Z' means 'Z is the mother of Y. 'Y & Z' means 'Y is married to Z' If 'N ! K % G & E + O % T ! V' and K has only one brother, then how is O related to V?
questions.explanation: It is given that, 'Y ! Z' means 'Z is the sister of Y' 'Y + Z' means 'Z is the daughter of Y's son' 'Y % Z' means 'Z is the mother of Y. 'Y & Z' means 'Y is married to Z' The given statement is: 'N ! K % G & E + O % T ! V' After decoding the statement, we get: K is the sister of N. K has only one brother. G is the mother of K. G is married to E. O is the daughter of E's son. T is the mother of O. V is the sister of T. Thus, O is the niece of V.

questions.question: In the following question, three statements are given and two conclusions are given below these statements. Choose the option which shows the conclusions which logically follow from the given statements, disregarding commonly known facts. Statements: Some desks are planks. All battens are desks. Only a few boards are desks. Conclusions: I. Some battens can be planks. II. Some boards are not desks.
questions.explanation: The given statements are, Some desks are planks. All battens are desks. Only a few boards are desks. The basic minimum overlapping diagram and another possible diagram are, Here, some battens can be planks and some boards are not desks. Hence, both conclusions I and II follow.

questions.question: In a class of 63 students, 31 passed in subject A, 29 passed in subject B, and 8 failed in both the subjects. How many students passed in both the subjects?
questions.explanation: Number of students passed in either subject A or subject B = 63 - 8 = 55 Number of students passed in both subjects = 31 + 29 - 55 = 60 - 55 = 5

questions.question: The positions of which of the following letters will remain unchanged in the word SUNSHINE if all letters are arranged in alphabetical order?
options: The letters that appear after N
The vowel that appears before F
The vowel that appears between H and N
questions.explanation: The given word is SUNSHINE. If all letters are arranged in alphabetical order, then we get: SUNSHINE EHINNSSU So, the positions of none of the letters remains unchanged.

questions.question: Select the combination of letters that when sequentially placed in the blanks of the given series will complete the series. kc_t_d_k_hs_df_eh_e_fk_h_e_f
options: h, e, g, d, e, k, r, d, f, q, d
h, e, f, d, e, k, r, d, f, q, d
h, e, g, d, e, k, s, d, f, q, d
h, e, f, d, e, k, s, d, f, q, d
questions.explanation: kchtedf kdhsedf kehredf kfhqedf The series has an alphabetical series of c, d, e and f. Secondly, the series has a reverse alphabetical series of t, s, r and q. So, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the number from among the given options that can replace the question mark (?) in the following series. 78, 82, 73, 89, ?
Which number has a digital root equal to 9?
questions.explanation: Option 1: 6 + 5 + 4 + 3 + 1 + 2 = 21, digital root = 2 + 1 = 3 Option 2: 7 + 7 + 4 + 8 + 5 + 2 = 33, digital root = 3 + 3 = 6 Option 3: 8 + 3 + 2 + 7 + 7 + 2 = 29, digital root = 2 + 9 = 11 = 2 Option 4: 9 + 1 + 8 + 3 + 4 + 2 = 27, digital root = 2 + 7 =9

questions.question: Direction: In an amusement park, the six ropeways viz., A, B, C, D, E and F have different lengths. A is shorter than B and D. F is longer than C and E. The lengths of two different ropeways vary between D and C. Not more than two ropeways are longer than A. Which of the following ropeways is the third shortest ropeway?
questions.explanation: In an amusement park, the six ropeways viz., A, B, C, D, E and F have different lengths. Not more than two ropeways are longer than A. A is shorter than B and D. Case 1: B > D > A > ___> ___> ___. Case 2: D > B > A > ___> ___> ___. The lengths of two different ropeways vary between D and C. Case 1: B > D > A > ___> C > ___. Case 2: D > B > A > C > ___> ___. F is longer than C and E. Thus, case 2 is invalid. Therefore, the final sequence is: B > D > A > F > C > E. Ropeway F is the third shortest among all.

questions.question: Which of the following letters will replace the question mark(?) in the given series? B, G, L, Q, V, ?
If + means x, ÷ means - , - means ÷ and x means +, what will come in the place of question mark (?) in the given equation? 39 x 217 + 3 ÷ 450 - 18 = ?
questions.explanation: If + means x, ÷ means - , - means ÷ and x means +, then the equation will be: 39 + 217 x 3 - 450 ÷ 18 = ? 39 + 651 - 450 ÷ 18 = ? 39 + 651 - 25 = ? 690 - 25 = ? So, ? = 665

questions.question: 'Manas National Park' is related to 'Assam' in the same way 'Silent Valley National Park' is related to ____________.
questions.explanation: 'Manas National Park' is in Assam. In the same way, 'Silent Valley National Park' is in Kerala.

questions.question: In a certain code language, "MOLE" is written as "4" and "PURPOSE" is written as "7". What will be the code for "MULTITUDINAL" in that code language?
questions.explanation: Number of letters in MOLE= 4 Number of letters in PURPOSE =7 So, the code for MULTITUDINAL= number of letters in MULTITUDINAL= 12.

questions.question: If in a certain code, 'BRIDGE' is written as 'ACQSHJCEFHDF', what is the code of POWER?
questions.explanation: Each letter is coded with its immediate neighbours in the alphabetical series, i.e., both the preceding and succeeding letters. The preceding and succeeding letters of B are A and C, R are Q and S, and so on. So, code for P O W E R = OQ NP VX DF QS

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Greater Flamingo Sanctuary in Tamil Nadu: i. The Greater Flamingo Sanctuary at Dhanushkodi lies within the Gulf of Mannar Biosphere Reserve and serves as an important stopover along the Central Asian Flyway. ii. The sanctuary spans over 5000 hectares and primarily consists of grassland ecosystems with minimal coastal or mangrove presence.
options: Both i and ii are correct
Neither i nor ii is correct
questions.explanation: The Tamil Nadu state government notified the establishment of the Greater Flamingo Sanctuary at Dhanushkodi in Ramanathapuram on 524.78 hectares of land on the occasion of World Environment Day. The Greater Flamingo Sanctuary, located within the ecologically sensitive Gulf of Mannar Biosphere Reserve, is a critical stopover along the Central Asian Flyway. This region supports 128 bird species, including Greater and Lesser Flamingos. The area features diverse ecosystems - sand dunes, marshes, and mangrove forests like Avicennia and Rhizophora, which stabilise the coastline and provide habitats for marine life, including fish, crustaceans, molluscs, and nesting sea turtles. The notification issued by additional chief secretary Supriya Sahu aims to safeguard this biodiversity hotspot and promote responsible tourism, benefiting local communities in Ramanathapuram.

questions.question: Which specific constitutional provision in India allows for the creation of Autonomous District Councils (ADCs) to protect tribal traditions in states like Assam and Meghalaya?
questions.explanation: Sixth Schedule It provides for the establishment of Autonomous District Councils (ADCs) in certain tribal areas of Northeast India such as Assam, Meghalaya, Tripura, and Mizoram. These councils have legislative, administrative, and judicial powers to protect tribal customs, land rights, and local governance. Fourth Schedule It deals with the allocation of seats in the Council of States (Rajya Sabha), not tribal administration. Fifth Schedule It deals with the administration of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes in other parts of India (mainly central and peninsular India), but it does not provide for Autonomous District Councils like the Sixth Schedule. Seventh Schedule It divides subjects between the Union and the States through Union List, State List, and Concurrent List. Further insights: • Sixth Schedule areas have councils with powers similar to mini-legislatures • ADCs can make laws on land, forests, and social customs • Meghalaya has multiple Autonomous District Councils like Khasi Hills and Garo Hills • The Governor has special powers regarding administration of Sixth Schedule areas • The Sixth Schedule is unique to certain Northeast India due to its distinct tribal diversity

questions.question: Given below are two statements marked as Assertion and Reason. Choose the correct option. Assertion (A): Fiscal deficit reflects the borrowing needs of the government. Reason (R): It represents excess of total expenditure over total receipts excluding borrowings.
options: Both A and R are true and R explains A
Both A and R are true but R does not explain A
A is true but R is false
A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is True: The Fiscal Deficit is essentially the gap in the government's budget. Since the government cannot spend money it doesn't have, this gap must be bridged through borrowing (from the RBI, commercial banks, or external sources). Therefore, the fiscal deficit is the most accurate indicator of the total borrowing requirements of the government. Reason (R) is True: The mathematical formula for Fiscal Deficit is: Fiscal Deficit = Total Expenditure - (Revenue Receipts + Non-debt Capital Receipts) In this equation, "Total Receipts" intentionally excludes borrowings. If we included borrowings in the receipts, the budget would always balance, and the deficit would appear as zero. Relationship Analysis Because the Fiscal Deficit is calculated specifically by excluding borrowings from the income side, the resulting "gap" represents exactly how much the government needs to borrow to cover its planned expenditures. Therefore, the Reason (R) provides the logical and mathematical explanation for why the Assertion (A) is a fact. Further insights: · Impact of High Fiscal Deficit: While borrowing allows for government spending on infrastructure, an excessively high deficit can lead to inflation, "crowding out" of private investment, and an increased burden of interest payments in future budgets. · Primary Deficit: This is another important metric, calculated by subtracting interest payments from the Fiscal Deficit. o Primary Deficit = Fiscal Deficit - Interest Payments It shows how much the government needs to borrow to meet its expenses other than its past debt obligations. · FRBM Act: In India, the Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management (FRBM) Act was enacted to institutionalize financial discipline and set targets for the government to reduce the fiscal deficit to a sustainable level.

questions.question: Which one of the following "Type of inflation-Description" pair is not matched properly?
options: Moderate Inflation- Inflation is in single digits, less than 10%
Stagflation- A fall in prices, a negative inflation rate
Disinflation- A falling rate of inflation
Creeping inflation- Rate of inflation slowly increases over time
questions.explanation: Type of InflationDescription StagflationCombination of stagnant economic growth, high unemployment, and high inflation. ShrinkflationWhen the price stays the same, but firms reduce the size of the good- effectively a price increase. DisinflationA fall in the inflation rate. It means prices are increasing at a slower rate. DeflationA fall in prices- a negative inflation rate. ReflationAct of stimulating the economy by increasing the money supply or by reducing taxes Creeping InflationWhen the rate of inflation slowly increases over time. For example, the inflation rate rises from 2% to 3%, to 4% a year. Walking/ Moderate InflationInflation is in single digits, less than 10%. Running InflationWhen inflation starts to rise at a significant rate. It is usually defined as a rate between 10% and 20% a year. Galloping inflationInflation rate of between 20% up to 1000%. HyperinflationThis is reserved for extreme forms of inflation- usually over 1,000% though there is no specific definition.

questions.question: Who was the first Indian woman to become the Governor of a state in India?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu was the first Indian woman to be appointed as the Governor of a state. She served as the Governor of the United Provinces (now Uttar Pradesh) from August 15, 1947, until her death in office on March 2, 1949. Her appointment coincided with India's independence, marking a significant milestone for women in Indian politics. Other options: Vijayalakshmi Pandit was a distinguished diplomat and the first woman to become the President of the United Nations General Assembly. She also served as the Governor of Maharashtra later, from 1962 to 1964. Sucheta Kriplani was the first woman to become the Chief Minister of an Indian state, serving as the head of the government of Uttar Pradesh from 1963 to 1967. Indira Gandhi was the first and, to date, the only female Prime Minister of India. Further Insights: Sarojini Naidu was a prominent figure in India's struggle for independence and was a close associate of Mahatma Gandhi. Beyond her political contributions, she was a celebrated poet, which earned her the title "The Nightingale of India" (Bharat Kokila). Her appointment as Governor in 1947 was a powerful symbol of the new role women were expected to play in an independent India.

questions.question: What does the custom of Chhawnghnawt involve, and during which festivals is it practised?
options: Community dancing performed by youth during Pawl Kut only
Mothers and children sitting on a memorial platform and feeding one another, observed during both Pawl Kut and Chapchar Kut
A ritual of offering rice-beer to elders, practised exclusively during Chapchar Kut
A day of complete rest observed after two days of festivities in Pawl Kut
questions.explanation: Pawl kut, a post-harvesting festival, is celebrated during December-January. Again, a mood of thanksgiving is evident, because the difficult task of tilling and harvesting is over. Community feasts are organised and dances are performed. Mothers with their children sit on memorial platform and feed one another. This custom, which is also performed during Chapchar Kut, is known as Chhawnghnawt. Drinking of rice-beer is also part of the festival. These two days of festivities are followed by a day of complete rest when no one goes out to work.

questions.question: Match the athletes with the medals they won for India at the 2022 Commonwealth Games (Birmingham): List I (Athletes) List II (Medals / Events - CWG 2022) A. Mirabai Chanu 1. Gold - Men's Triple Jump B. Achinta Sheuli 2. Gold - Women's 49 kg Weightlifting C. Eldhose Paul 3. Gold - Men's 73 kg Weightlifting D. Nikhat Zareen 4. Gold - Women's 50 kg Boxing
questions.explanation: Star Indian weightlifter Mirabai Chanu won India's first gold medal at the Birmingham Games, dominating the 49 kg category. Weightlifter Achinta Sheuli continued India's impressive run in the sport by clinching the gold medal in the men's 73 kg category. In a historic moment for Indian athletics, Eldhose Paul won the gold medal in the men's triple jump, leading an Indian 1-2 finish in the event. Boxer Nikhat Zareen capped a dominant performance at the games by winning the gold medal in the women's 50 kg (light flyweight) category.

questions.question: The dimensional formula of the universal gravitational constant G is
questions.explanation: From F = G m1 m2 / r2, we have G = F r2/(m1 m2). [M L T-2]·[L2]·[M-2] = M-1 L3 T-2. Further Insights: - SI unit of G is N·m2·kg-2; numerical value ≈ 6.674 X 10-11 N·m2·kg-2. - Dimension of g is L T-2; potential energy GMm/r has dimension M L2 T-2. - Escape speed v_e = √(2GM/R) is dimensionally L T-1.

questions.question: Consider the statements regarding Union Public Service Commission (UPSC). 1) The expenses of UPSC are charged on the Consolidated Fund of India. 2) The UPSC Chairperson can be removed by the President for misbehaviour only after the Supreme Court, on a reference, upholds the charge. 3) A person who has been a UPSC Member may be appointed to any other government post thereafter. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct (financial independence). Statement 2 is correct (Article 317; SC inquiry for "misbehaviour" grounds). Statement 3 is incorrect: Article 319 bars such employment; a Member may become UPSC/SPSC Chairperson, but no other government employment is allowed. Further Insights: - Constitutional base: Articles 315 - 323. - UPSC conducts exams for All-India Services and Central Services; advises on recruitment, promotions, and disciplinary matters. - Chairman/Member tenure: 6 years or until 65 years of age. - States have their own SPSC; Joint PSCs can be created for a group of States. - Failure to consult UPSC does not void a decision but must be explained when required.

questions.question: Which statement correctly describes the Champaran Satyagraha (1917)?
options: It was a nationwide agitation against the Rowlatt Act.
It targeted the tinkathia system of indigo cultivation in Bihar and used inquiry and legal pressure to secure relief.
It launched the Non-Cooperation Movement demanding Purna Swaraj.
It was centred on the salt tax and coastal marches in Gujarat.
questions.explanation: Champaran focused on oppressive indigo contracts (tinkathia) in Bihar; Gandhi led fact-finding, legal petitions, and negotiations, resulting in concessions. The Rowlatt Satyagraha was in 1919; Non-Cooperation in 1920; salt satyagraha in 1930. Further Insights: - Key associates included Rajendra Prasad, J.B. Kripalani, and Mahadev Desai. - Ahmedabad Mill Strike (1918) involved arbitration for textile workers; Kheda Satyagraha (1918) addressed revenue remission after crop failure. - Gandhi returned to India in 1915 and toured to understand local conditions before leading mass movements. - Champaran marked Gandhi's first major satyagraha in India and showcased his methods of inquiry, mobilization, and negotiation.

questions.question: India concluded its campaign at the World Boxing Futures Cup with five medals on March 15, 2026. Who clinched the only gold medal for India at this event?
questions.explanation: India concluded its campaign at the World Boxing Futures Cup with a haul of five medals, including one gold, on March 15, 2026. Competing across the Youth Olympic weight categories, the Indian U-19 men's and women's team delivered a series of assured performances. Leading the charge was Chandrika Pujari, who delivered a commanding performance in the women's 51 kg final to defeat Uzbekistan's Mardonova Nazokat with a unanimous decision to stand a top the podium. Three Indian boxers finished with silver medals after reaching the finals in their respective categories. Gunjan (48kg) went down to her opponent from England by a 5-0 decision, while Joyshree Devi (54kg) fought hard but lost 4-1 to a boxer from the United States. In the men's 50kg category, L. Ambekar Meetei also settled for silver after losing his final bout to Ukraine. Earlier in the tournament, Radhamani Longjam (57kg) had secured a bronze medal.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Pushkar Camel Fair of Rajasthan: Statement I: The Pushkar Mela is traditionally held during the Hindu lunar month of Kartik and is centered around the world's only prominent temple dedicated to Lord Brahma. Statement II: This fair serves as a unique intersection of cultural tourism where large-scale livestock trade of camels and horses occurs alongside religious pilgrimage rituals. Which of the following is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement I is correct The Pushkar Camel Fair is held during the Kartik month (October - November) and is closely associated with the Brahma Temple in Pushkar, which is one of the very few prominent temples dedicated to Lord Brahma. Statement II is correct The fair combines religious activities like holy bathing in Pushkar Lake with large-scale trading of camels and horses. It also attracts tourists for its cultural events, making it a major example of cultural tourism. Further insights: · Pushkar is located in Rajasthan near Ajmer. · Pilgrims take a holy dip in Pushkar Lake during Kartik Purnima. · It is one of the largest camel fairs in the world. · The fair includes folk music, dance, and competitions. · It boosts local economy through tourism and trade.

questions.question: Vijayalaya, the founder of the Chola dynasty, was the first feudatory of the __
questions.explanation: The founder of the Chola Empire was Vijayalaya, who was first feudatory of the Pallavas of Kanchi. He captured Tanjore in 850 A.D. He established a temple of goddess Nishumbhasudini (Durga) there. Aditya I succeeded Vijayalaya.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers forms the deepest gorge in the Indian Peninsular Plateau?
questions.explanation: The Narmada River is famous for flowing through a deep, narrow gorge known as the Marble Rocks (Bhedaghat) near Jabalpur in Madhya Pradesh. The Narmada flows through a Rift Valley-a lowland region that forms where Earth's tectonic plates move apart or where the crust drops between faults. This rift valley is situated between the Vindhya Range (North) and the Satpura Range (South). The Gorge: As the river cuts through the soft magnesium limestone (marble) of the region, it has carved out a spectacular gorge that is approximately 3 km long. Dhuandhar Falls: Just before entering the gorge, the river drops significantly to form the Dhuandhar Falls ("Smoke Cascade"), where the intensity of the water creates a mist-like appearance.

questions.question: Read the below statements marked as Assertion (A) and Reason (R). Mark the correct option: Assertion (A): The Brihadisvara Temple at Thanjavur is considered a masterpiece of Dravidian architecture. Reason (R): It features a towering vimana built entirely of granite, rising over 60 metres, constructed during the Chola dynasty.
options: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
Both A and R are true and R is not the correct explanation of A.
A is true, but R is false.
A is false, but R is true.
questions.explanation: Both the Assertion and the Reason are true, and R correctly explains A. The Brihadisvara Temple (built c. 1010 CE by Raja Raja Chola I) is indeed a pinnacle of Dravidian architecture. Its massive granite vimana - standing about 66 metres tall - is a defining example of the Chola architectural achievement, which is precisely what makes it a masterpiece of the Dravidian style.

questions.question: Which of the following crops are cultivated in Plantation agriculture? 1. Palm oil 2. Black Pepper 3. Rubber The correct option is:
questions.explanation: All these crops are part of Plantation Agriculture. Crops grown under Plantation Agriculture: 1. Spices: Cardamom, Black Pepper, Red Chilly 2. Edible oil: Coconut, Palm oil 3. Tea, Coffee, Rubber Features of Plantation Agriculture 1. Same crop is grown over a large area 2. Highly capital and labor intensive 3. Mostly Commercial crops are grown

questions.question: The following Age-Sex Pyramid, of Australia, shows which kind of population?
questions.explanation: Australia's age-sex pyramid is bell shaped and tapered towards the top. This shows birth and death rates are almost equal leading to a near constant population. A triangular shaped pyramid with a wide base and is typical of less developed countries. These have larger populations in lower age groups due to high birth rates, and show an expanding population. A pyramid with a narrow base and a tapered top shows low birth and death rates. The population growth in developed countries is usually zero or negative. This is the declining population. Image credit: NCERT

questions.question: Why did the 86th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2002 add a new Fundamental Duty to the Indian Constitution?
options: To introduce compulsory military service for citizens
To ensure parental responsibility towards children's education
To expand the scope of Directive Principles of State Policy
To strengthen the powers of local self-government institutions
questions.explanation: The 86th Amendment: · Inserted Article 21A → Right to Education · Added Article 51A(k) → Fundamental Duty of parents/guardians,To ensure that parents or guardians provide opportunities for education to children aged 6 - 14 years Further insights · The 86th Amendment strengthened the rights - duties balance in the Constitution. · Article 51A(k) places moral responsibility on parents and guardians. · Right to Education is a justiciable Fundamental Right. · Fundamental Duties are non-justiciable but constitutionally significant. · Education reform reflects India's shift towards human capital development.

questions.question: With reference to famous books and their authors, consider the following statements. Statement 1. Train to Pakistan is a Partition-era novel authored by Khushwant Singh. Statement 2. The Namesake is an autobiographical memoir written by Jhumpa Lahiri.
options: Neither Statement 1 nor Statement 2 is correct
questions.explanation: Train to Pakistan (1956) is indeed a Partition novel by Khushwant Singh. The Namesake (2003) is a work of fiction (a novel) by Jhumpa Lahiri, not a memoir. Further Insights: - Khushwant Singh also wrote I Shall Not Hear the Nightingale. - Jhumpa Lahiri's non-fiction includes In Other Words (Italian original). - Amitav Ghosh authored The Glass Palace and The Shadow Lines. - Vikram Seth wrote A Suitable Boy, a landmark Indian English novel.

questions.question: The term "Half Nelson" is associated with:
questions.explanation: The term "Half Nelson" is a fundamental grappling hold used in Wrestling. It is a pinning hold where the wrestler passes their hand under the opponent's arm from behind and places their palm on the back of the opponent's neck. By applying upward pressure, the wrestler can control the opponent's head and upper body, often using it to turn them onto their back for a pin. Explanation of Other Options: Judo: Judo terminology is primarily Japanese. Common terms include Ippon (full point), Waza-ari (half point), O-goshi (major hip throw), and Ne-waza (ground techniques). Kabaddi: Terms in Kabaddi include 'raider', 'anti-raider', 'cant', 'bonus line', and 'super tackle'. Boxing: Boxing terms include 'jab', 'hook', 'uppercut', 'knockout', and 'clinch'. Further insights: The "Half Nelson" is one of the most basic and effective holds taught to amateur wrestlers. More advanced variations of the hold include the "Full Nelson" (which is illegal in amateur wrestling due to the risk of injury) and the "Three-quarter Nelson." The hold's name is believed to have originated in the 19th century, possibly named after a wrestler or as a reference to Admiral Horatio Nelson, though the exact etymology is debated.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Bhakti Movement: Statement I: It advocated for a personal, emotional bond between the devotee and a personal God, often bypassing formal rituals. Statement II: The movement's saints primarily used Sanskrit to ensure their message reached the scholarly elite across the subcontinent. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
questions.explanation: Statement I is Correct: The core philosophy of the Bhakti movement was the "Path of Devotion." It emphasized that salvation (Moksha) could be attained through intense love and devotion to God, rather than through complex Vedic sacrifices or rigid priestly rituals. Statement II is Incorrect: One of the most significant impacts of the Bhakti movement was its use of regional languages (vernaculars) like Hindi, Bengali, Marathi, Tamil, and Punjabi. This was a deliberate choice by saints like Kabir, Mirabai, and Guru Nanak to make spiritual teachings accessible to the common masses who did not understand Sanskrit. · Tulsidas used Awadhi (Ramcharitmanas). · Mirabai used Rajasthani/Braj Bhasha. · Shankaradeva used Assamese. · Jnaneshwar and Tukaram used Marathi. This linguistic shift was a primary reason for the movement's widespread popularity and the development of modern Indian languages.

questions.question: Which of the following scientists received the Nobel Prize in Physics 2025 for the discovery of macroscopic quantum mechanical tunnelling and energy quantisation in an electric circuit?
options: Michel H. Devoret, Niels Bohr, Richard Feynman
Brian Josephson, Paul Dirac, Erwin Schr'dinger
Richard Feynman, Werner Heisenberg, Max Planck
John Clarke, Michel H. Devoret, John M. Martinis
questions.explanation: Three United States-based scientists, John Clarke, Michel H Devoret, and John M. Martinis, received the Nobel Prize in Physics for the discovery of macroscopic quantum mechanical tunnelling and energy quantisation in an electric circuit, the Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences said in a statement. The Academy said that a major question in physics is the maximum size of a system that can demonstrate quantum mechanical effects. 'This year's Nobel Prize laureates conducted experiments with an electrical circuit in which they demonstrated both quantum mechanical tunnelling and quantised energy levels in a system big enough to be held in the hand,' it said. The total prize amount of 11 million Swedish kronor will be shared equally among the laureates. Winners of the Nobel Prize in Physics: John Clarke: Born in 1942 in Cambridge, UK. Earned his PhD in 1968 from the University of Cambridge. Currently a Professor at the University of California, Berkeley, US. Michel H Devoret:Born in 1953 in Paris, France. Received his PhD in 1982 from Paris-Sud University, France. Serves as a Professor at Yale University, New Haven, Connecticut, and at the University of California, Santa Barbara, US. John M Martinis:Born in 1958. Obtained his PhD in 1987 from the University of California, Berkeley. Currently a Professor at the University of California, Santa Barbara, US. The Academy said that this year's Nobel Prize in Physics has provided opportunities for developing the next generation of quantum technology, including, quantum cryptography, quantum computers, and quantum sensors. The Nobel Prize is a set of prestigious international awards given annually to individuals and organizations for outstanding achievements in physics, chemistry, physiology or medicine, literature, and peace. The Sveriges Riksbank Prize in Economic Sciences in Memory of Alfred Nobel was added in 1968.

questions.question: Which of the following climates(as per Koppen Classification) is characterized by high temperature throughout the year and annual rainfall exceeding 2000 mm?
questions.explanation: Tropical Rainforest climate (Af) is marked by: -High temperatures all year (≥ 18C) -Very high annual rainfall (typically > 2000 mm) Examples include Amazon Basin, Congo Basin, parts of Kerala and Northeast India. Other options: Aw (Tropical Savannah) → distinct wet and dry seasons BWh (Hot Desert) → extremely low rainfall Csa (Mediterranean) → dry summers, mild wet winters

questions.question: Slug, Pond snail, cuttlefish can be classified as _______
questions.explanation: Mollusca is a large phylum of invertebrate animals whose members are known as molluscs or mollusks. The three most universal features defining modern molluscs are a mantle with a significant cavity used for breathing and excretion, the presence of a radula (except for bivalves), and the structure of the nervous system. Example - Slug, Pond snail, cuttlefish

questions.question: Identify the chemical name for Ge.
questions.explanation: Germanium (Ge) is a chemical element between silicon and tin in Group 14 (IVa) of the periodic table. It is a silvery-gray metalloid, intermediate in properties between the metals and the nonmetals. Gallium - Ga; Gold - Au and Gadolinium - Gd.

questions.question: A seller offers a discount of 15% if the total selling price of different products purchased by a single customer is below Rs. 4000, and a discount of 18% if the total selling value of all the products exceeds Rs. 4000. A shirt costing Rs. 1250 was sold to Ritu after earning a margin of 8% and also a jacket costing Rs. 2000 was sold to him after a margin of 6%. How much does Ritu have to pay (in Rs.) for the final settlement of the bill?
Two sides of a triangle are 12 cm and 16 cm long. If the length of the third side of the triangle is 'X' cm, then X:
questions.explanation: The range of X is, (16 - 12) < X < (16 + 12) 4 < X < 28

questions.question: If sin 42° = p/q, then the value of the sec 42° + sin 48° is :
questions.explanation: Sin 42° = p/q H2 = P2 + B2 (Pythagoras theorem where H = Hypotenuse, P = Perpendicular and B = Base) Sin θ = P/H Cos θ = B/H Tan θ = P/B Sin 42° = P/H P = p units and H = q units Putting value of P and H in Pythagoras theorem B2 = H2 - P2 B2 = q2 - p2 Sec 42° = H/B = q/√(q2 - p2) Cos 42° = sin 48° = √(q2 - p2)/q Sec 42° + sin 48° = q/√(q2 - p2) + √(q2 - p2)/q => [q2 + (q2 - p2)]/[q (√(q2 - p2)] => [2q2 - p2]/ [q√(q2 - p2)]

questions.question: The diameter of a sphere is (√3)/2 cm. The surface area (in sq. cm) of sphere lies between:
questions.explanation: Diameter of sphere = (√3)/2 cm Radius of sphere = (√3)/4 cm Surface area = 4π * r2 = 4 * (22/7) * (3/16) = 66/28 = 33/14 = 2.35 = between 2 and 3.

questions.question: What is the area of the region created by the two linear equations 6x + y = 18 and 2x - y = - 2, bounded by these lines and the x-axis?
questions.explanation: lies on x -axis, so, y = 0, 6x + 0 = 18 => x = 3, lies on (3, 0) 2x - 0 = -2 => x = -1, lies on (-1, 0) 6x + y = 18 ..(i) 2x - y = -2 .. (ii) From (i) and (ii) x = 2, y = 6, lies on (2, 6) Hence, the required area = ½ * base * height = 1/2 * 6 * 4 [base is from -1 to 3 = 4 units, height = y] = 12 sq units

questions.question: The area of the base of a cylinder is 77 cm2. Find the volume of cylinder which has height 14.28% more than its radius. (Take π = 22/7)
questions.explanation: Area of base of cylinder = πr2 = 77 Height = Volume =

questions.question: In a 700 liters mixture, quantity of milk and water is in the ratio 9: 5 respectively. When 'n' liters of water is added to the mixture, ratio of milk and water becomes 6: 5 respectively. What is the value of 'n'?
questions.explanation: Quantity of mixture = 700 liters Then, quantity of milk = 700 * 9/(9 + 5) = 450 liters And, quantity of water = 700 - 450 = 250 liters Now, 450: (250 + n) = 6: 5 n = 125

questions.question: A cone of radius 28 cm and height 45 cm is melted and recast into some cuboids of dimension 40 cm * 21 cm * 11 cm. Find the number of cuboids made from the cone.
questions.explanation: Radius of cone = 28 cm Height of cone = 45 cm Dimension of cuboid = 40 cm * 21 cm * 11 cm Let the number of cuboids = 'n' 1/3 * 22/7 * 28 *28 * 45 = n * 40 * 21 * 11 n = (22*4*28*15)/(40*21*11) n = 4

questions.question: If tan2 A = 2 - B2, where A is an acute angle, then find the value of tan3 A_________sin A + 1_________cos A
The income of a share holder remains unchanged although the rate of commission is increased from 3 percent to 10 percent. The percentage of slump in bond is:
questions.explanation: Let the bond value changes from Rs.r to Rs. y. 3% of r = 10% of y 3r/100 = 10y/100 y =3r/10 Thus, the change in the bond r - 3r/10 = 7r/10 Percentage of slump in the bond = (7r/10)/(r) x 100 = 70%

questions.question: The unit digit of 3204 is:
questions.explanation: We know that, 34 = 81 3(4)51 = 8151 So, unit digit will be 1.

questions.question: The length of common chord of two circles intersecting each other such that each pass through the center of the other, is Y√3 cm. If the radius of both the circles is equal, then find the radius.
questions.explanation: We know that when two equal circles of radius R cm intersects each other such that each pass through the center of the other, then the length of common chord be R√3 cm. Therefore, radius of circles = Y cm

questions.question: One-sixth of a number is 3 more than one-ninth of the same number. Find the half of the number.
questions.explanation: Let number = N According to the question: (N/6) - (N/9) = 3 N/18 = 3 N = 54 Half of the number = N/2 = 54/2 = 27

questions.question: If a group of 'X' people can complete a work in 'X' days and if 'Y' number of people can not cooperate with the remaining ones due to some personal reason and the work is now completed in 'Z' days, then which of the following option are true?
questions.explanation: Using the MDH formula, we have X * X = (X - Y) * Z X2 = XZ - YZ X2 - XZ = -YZ XZ - X2 = YZ Y = (XZ - X2)/Z Y = X(Z - X)/Z.

questions.question: A train has a speed of 72 km/hr. Due stoppages it took 9 hours to cover a distance of 504 km. For how much time did it stop per hour?
questions.explanation: Without stoppages, time taken = 504/72 = 7 hours. Since the train took 9 hours, it stopped for 2 hours in between Average stoppage time = 2/9 hours = 120/9 minutes = 13 1/3 minutes = 13 minutes 20 seconds.

questions.question: If Alisha and Priya entered into a partnership with Rs 3,000 each and after 4 months Alisha withdrew Rs 1,000 and Priya invested Rs 1,000 more. After how many months from beginning of the partnership has Avantika joined with a investment of Rs 6,000 such that if the profit at the end of the year is Rs 48,000, she receives a share of Rs 12,000?
questions.explanation: Let Avantika enter after '12 −x' months. So her amount was invested for a period of 'x' months. Profit will be shared in the ratio (3000*4+2000*8):(3000*4+4000*8):(6000x) or, 14:22:3x Given, (3x/(36 + 3x))*48000 = 12000 x/(12 + x) = 1/4 4x = 12 + x x = 4 Avantika entered after (12 - 4) or, 8 months.

questions.question: 24 boys can do a work in 20 hours. If 8 boys deny to work, then how many hours required to do the work?
What is the compound ratio of 15: 8, 27: 16, 64: 135, and 2: 5?
If x = √5 - 2_________√5 + 2 , then the value of x2 + x-2 is :
The difference between simple interest and compound interest on Rs.8000 at 5% per annum for 36 months is -
questions.explanation: 36 months = (36/12) years = 3 years Difference between CI and SI for 3 years = 3PR2/10000 + P(R/100)3 = 3 * 8000 * 52/10000 + 8000 * (5/100)3 = Rs.61

questions.question: In the given figure, ∠ABO = 15°, ∠ACO = 10°, find the measure of ∠BOC.
The average weight of 12 persons of a family got increased by 2.6 kg, when a new person joins the family in place of a person weighing 49 kg. find the weight of new person.
questions.explanation: Total increased weight = 12 * 2.6 = 31.2 kg Weight of new person = 49 + 31.2 = 80.2 kg

questions.question: ∆ABC is an equilateral triangle and AD is perpendicular to BC, where D lies on BC. If AD = 4√3 cm, then what is the perimeter (in cm) of ∆ABC?
questions.explanation: Since, ∆ABC is an equilateral triangle, so AD = (√3/2) * side of triangle 4√3 = (√3/2) * side of triangle Side of triangle = 8 cm Perimeter of triangle = 8 + 8 + 8 = 24 cm

questions.question: Which of the following schemes will give more discount to a customer? Scheme A: Buy 21 get 10 free. Scheme B: Buy 25 get 12 free.
questions.explanation: Scheme A: Discount% = Free * 100/Total = 10 * 100/21 = 47.62% Scheme B: Discount% = 12 * 100/25 = 48%

questions.question: Which among the following sentences uses the article (if required) before the word 'hour' correctly?
options: It took a hour to finish the work.
It took an hour to finish the work.
It took the hour to finish the work.
It took hour to finish the work.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, (b) is the best fit because 'hour' begins with a silent 'h', making the initial sound a vowel sound, so 'an' is correctly used. Option (a) is incorrect as 'a' is not used before vowel sounds, (c) incorrectly uses 'the' without specific reference, and (d) omits the required article. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into three segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. If the sentence is error free, mark 'No error' as your answer. She usually reads a/ book in the evening and/ finished it by bedtime.
questions.explanation: Option (d) contains the error. The tense in the second part of the sentence should be consistent with the first part of the sentence. Since the first part uses the present tense ("reads"), the second part should also be in the present tense. The sentence describes the routine of the subject "she", so the simple present tense would be appropriate here. Therefore, "finished" [simple past] should be corrected to "finishes" [simple present]. Hence, (d) is the correct answer. The corrected sentence: She usually reads a book in the evening and finishes it by bedtime.

questions.question: Find the part of the sentence that contains an error: The safety inspector suggested to install additional lighting (1)/ in the factory's main production area to reduce (2)/ the risk of accidents and to improve (3)/ the overall working conditions for the employees. (4)
options: the overall working conditions for the employees (4)
in the factory's main production area to reduce (2)
The safety inspector suggested to install additional lighting (1)
the risk of accidents and to improve (3)
questions.explanation: The error in part (1) is in the grammatical structure that follows the verb "suggested." The verb "suggest" should not be followed by the "to-infinitive" ("to + base verb"). Instead, it should be followed by either a gerund (-ing form of the verb) or a "that" clause (often using the subjunctive mood). Incorrect: He suggested to install... Correct (with gerund): He suggested installing... Correct (with "that" clause): He suggested that they install... The corrected sentence should read: "The safety inspector suggested installing additional lighting in the factory's main production area to reduce the risk of accidents and to improve the overall working conditions for the employees." OR "The safety inspector suggested that they install additional lighting in the factory's main production area to reduce the risk of accidents and to improve the overall working conditions for the employees." Hence, option (c) is the correct answer. Note: Gerund: A Gerund is a verb form that functions as a noun, ending in -ing. A gerund can be either the subject or object of a sentence in English. Example: "Everybody enjoyed her dancing and singing at the party." The words 'dancing' and 'singing' are gerunds. Infinitive: The infinitive is the base form of a verb, often preceded by to. Consider the sentences given below: · To err is human. [like a noun, the infinitive 'to err' is the subject of the verb 'is'] · My father likes to cook. [like a noun, the infinitive 'to cook' is the object of the verb 'likes'] · To vote in elections is our right. [The infinitive 'to vote', like a noun, is the subject of the verb 'is', but, like a verb, it also takes an object (elections)] · Radha went to the lake to fetch water. [The infinitive 'to fetch', like a noun, is the object of the verb 'went', but, like a verb, it also takes an object (water)] · The above sentences illustrate various ways in which infinitives are used. It can both act as a noun and a verb.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of personification?
options: The snow was a white blanket covering the town.
Her voice is music to his ears.
questions.explanation: Personification is giving human qualities, emotions, or actions to inanimate objects or abstract ideas. a): This is personification. A camera is an inanimate object and cannot feel the human emotion of "love." The phrase means she looks very good in photographs. b): This is a metaphor, comparing a person who stays up late to an owl. c): This is a metaphor, equating snow with a blanket. d): This is also a metaphor, stating that her voice is music. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of simile?
options: The child's laughter was like a bubbling brook.
The stars winked in the night sky.
questions.explanation: A simile is a figure of speech that compares two different things using the words "like" or "as." a): This is a metaphor, directly equating the assignment with a breeze to imply it was easy. b): This is a perfect simile. It compares the sound of laughter to the sound of a brook using the word "like." c): This is personification, giving the stars the human action of winking. d): This is a metaphor. A figure of speech that makes a direct comparison by stating one thing is another, without using "like" or "as". Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that uses a homonym of the italicized word: She will address the issue in the meeting.
options: He will address the gathering tomorrow.
Please address the problem in front of your neighborhood.
His address was difficult to locate
They will address a very important issue today.
questions.explanation: Homonyms are words that are spelled and pronounced the same but have different meanings. In the original sentence, "address" means to deal with or speak about an issue. Options (a), (b), and (c) also use "address" in the sense of dealing with, speaking about, or discussing something. However, in option (c), "address" means the location or place where someone lives or can be found. Since this meaning is completely different from the original one. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. The entire blame for crime cannot be led at the doors of the State.
questions.explanation: 'Led' is the past participle form of verb “lead” which means to guide on a way especially by going in advance. Thus, it does not suit the context of the sentence. 'Laid', the participle form of the verb 'lay' means to put or set down should be used here. 'Lay at someone's door' is the idiom that means 'to assign blame or responsibility to one for something'. This fits the context of the sentence. Also, the modal verb (cannot) should be followed by the base form of the verb. So, been (past participle) and being (present participle) of the verb 'be' cannot be used here. So, only option c) (be laid at the doors) can improve the underlined part of the given sentence. Hence, option c) is correct. Correct sentence: The entire blame for crime cannot be laid at the doors of the State.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank 1.
questions.explanation: Kindled: Ignited or sparked; the act of starting a fire or metaphorically, arousing emotions or enthusiasm. Concerned: Anxious, troubled, or interested in a particular issue or matter. Assumed: Taken for granted or accepted without proof; taking on a role or responsibility without being formally appointed. Opposed: In conflict or resistance to something; being against or in disagreement with a particular idea, action, or person. The sentence with the blank discusses the leap year as a deviation from the regular 365 days. Among the given options, the word 'opposed' fits well in the context of the sentence as it signifies a contrast or resistance to the regular system and indicates that an additional day is added in the leap year in opposition to the standard 365 days. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank 2.
questions.explanation: Revolve: To orbit around a central point or to move in a circular path. In the context of celestial bodies, it refers to the motion around an axis. Form: To shape or create something; the structure or arrangement of an object or idea. Change: To undergo a transformation or become different; a shift or alteration in form, nature, or characteristics. Demonstrate: To show or prove something clearly through evidence or action. The sentence describes the Earth's movement around the Sun, which is a circular motion. Among the given options, the word 'revolve' precisely captures this idea, emphasizing the circular path that celestial bodies, like Earth, take in their orbit around a central point. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank 3.
questions.explanation: Extending: Stretching or prolonging something in terms of length or duration. Normalising: Making something conform to a standard or norm. Abating: Decreasing or reducing in intensity, degree, or amount. Occurring: Taking place or happening. The sentence with the blank talks about crop cycles and seasons starting at different times, indicating a change in their occurrence. Among the given options, the word 'occurring' is the most suitable choice as it conveys the idea that these events could take place or happen at different times if the adjustment of a leap year was not made. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank 4.
questions.explanation: Frivolous: Lacking seriousness or importance; trivial, silly. Precise: Exact, accurate, or clearly defined; a high level of accuracy and attention to detail. Utopian: Idealistic or impractical; a vision or idea that is often considered perfect but may be unattainable in reality. Ambiguous: Having multiple meanings or interpretations, unclear, or open to different understandings. The sentence suggests that the leap year was introduced to make the calendar more accurate. Among the given options, the word 'precise' accurately conveys the idea of making something exact and accurate, with the purpose of introducing a leap year to align the Julian calendar more closely with the solar year. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank 5.
questions.explanation: Necessarily: Inevitably or as a necessary consequence. Keenly: Intensely or eagerly. Slightly: To a small degree or extent. Adamantly: Firmly, resolutely, or stubbornly. The sentence with the blank emphasises the difference between the calendar year and the solar year, stating that the Julian calendar year is a little bit longer than the solar year. Among the given options, the word 'slightly' is the appropriate choice as it indicates a small difference, reinforcing the notion that the additional time is minimal. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the correct ANTONYM of 'Suave'.
questions.explanation: Suave/ elegant - charming and confident. Crude - unrefined and rude. naïve - innocent. Hence, option (b) is the right answer.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. Temporary police force.
questions.explanation: 'Posse' means temporary police force. Hence, (d) is the correct answer. 'Navy' means a country's sea force. 'Army' means a large, highly organised military force. 'Brood' means a family of birds or other young animals produced at one hatching or birth.

questions.question: Pick the spelling that is correct for a person who writes dictionaries.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, lexicographer is the correct spelling referring to a person who compiles or writes dictionaries. The other options contain spelling errors: lexicografar replaces the ending with an incorrect vowel pattern, lexicografer changes "ph" to "f", and lexicografur alters the final vowel and consonant sequence. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. Asseverate
questions.explanation: Asseverate - to declare something earnestly or solemnly; to affirm positively. Recant - to say that one no longer holds an opinion or belief; to withdraw a statement. Since asseverate means to declare something to be true, its direct opposite is to formally withdraw that statement, which is to recant. Hence, option b) is the correct answer. Assert - to state a fact or belief confidently and forcefully; a synonym of asseverate. Announce - to make a formal public statement about a fact, occurrence, or intention. Believe - to accept that (something) is true, especially without proof.

questions.question: A sentence is provided in indirect speech. Select the correct direct speech option. The coach ordered the players to remain on the field until the final whistle was blown.
options: The coach said, "You remain on the field until the final whistle was blown."
The coach said to the players, "Remain on the field until the final whistle is blown."
The coach ordered, "Remain on the field until the final whistle was blown."
The coach said to the players, "You remain on the field until the final whistle is blown."
questions.explanation: The correct option is (b). When converting an indirect sentence expressing an order or command into direct speech, the structure changes to an imperative form. The verb "ordered someone to.." is typically expressed as "said to someone, '[command]'". The verb in the command remains in base form, and the clause inside the quotation is expressed in a natural present form. Given sentence: The coach ordered the players to remain on the field until the final whistle was blown. So, the correct direct speech is: The coach said to the players, "Remain on the field until the final whistle is blown." Option (a) is incorrect because "You remain" does not form a proper command and uses reported tense ("was blown"). Option (c) is incorrect because "was blown" retains the reported past form inside the quotation. Option (d) is incorrect because "You remain" is not an appropriate imperative structure for a command. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Be snowed under
questions.explanation: The idiom 'be snowed under' means to have so much work that you have problems dealing with it all; overwhelmed with work. Option D best reflects this meaning.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. Afterwards, they were asked separately how much they liked this other person and how much they thought the other person liked them back. B. Researchers invited college students to come to a research laboratory where they chatted with another person they did not know. C. The researchers found that most students said they liked the other person, but thought the other person liked them less. D. The length of the conversation did not matter as students felt this way after talking for a couple of minutes, but also when they chatted for 45 min.
questions.explanation: Sentence B introduces the context of the study, explaining the who, where, and what - researchers invited students to participate in a study where they interacted with someone unfamiliar. This provides the background needed to understand the rest of the passage. Sentence A logically follows sentence B by describing what happened after the conversation introduced in 'B'. It explains the next step in the research: asking participants about their feelings toward the other person and vice versa. The use of "afterwards" links it directly to the scenario in 'B'. After the questioning process is explained in sentence A, sentence C presents the findings of the study: the students tended to like the other person but assumed the feeling was not mutual. This provides the outcome of the survey described in 'A' and logically continues the narrative. Sentence D adds a detail about the findings in 'C', specifying that the students' perceptions were consistent regardless of the conversation's duration. It serves as a concluding point, reinforcing the results discussed in 'C' by adding a factor (time) that did not influence the outcome. So, the correct order is BACD. Hence, option (b) is the answer.

questions.question: In the following question, the first and the last parts of the sentence/passage are numbered 1 and 6. The rest of the sentence/passage is split into four parts - P, Q, R, and S. These four parts are not given in their proper order. Read the sentence/passage and find the most logical order of the parts between 1 and 6. 1: Rock climbing is a challenging sport that involves ascending a rock face. P. Essential safety gear, such as ropes, harnesses, and helmets, is used to prevent injury. Q. It requires a combination of physical strength, endurance, and mental focus. R. The goal is to reach the top of a formation or the end of a pre-defined route. S. Climbers must constantly solve physical puzzles to find the best handholds and footholds. 6: It offers both a great physical workout and a deep sense of personal accomplishment.
questions.explanation: The passage describes the key aspects of rock climbing as a sport. 1 introduces the sport. R logically starts by defining the primary "goal" of the activity. Q follows by explaining the general skills "required" to achieve the goal from R. S provides a specific example of the "mental focus" and problem-solving aspect mentioned in Q. P introduces the topic of safety equipment, which is a practical consideration for the activity described. 6 concludes by summarizing the rewards of the sport. Thus, the correct and logical sequence is RQSP. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. Had the patient been informed about the change in dosage before discharge?
options: Did someone inform the patient about the change in dosage before discharge?
Had someone informed the patient about the change in dosage before discharge?
Had the doctor informed the patient about the change in dosage before discharge?
Was someone informing the patient about the change in dosage before discharge?
questions.explanation: Option (b) is correct. The sentence is in PAST PERFECT interrogative passive: "Had ... been informed?" In active voice, past perfect passive changes into HAD + subject + past participle. Since the doer is unknown, SOMEONE is the safest subject. So the correct form is HAD SOMEONE INFORMED. • Option (a): changes the tense to simple past. • Option (c): adds "the doctor" as the doer, but no such doer is given in the original sentence. • Option (d): changes the tense to past continuous.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. _________ going to be the topper in the upcoming semester.
questions.explanation: · The correct answer is: (b) It is I who am. · "It is" is always followed by a subjective pronoun. In this case, the subjective pronoun that will be used is "I". · When a sentence has the subject "I", the verb is placed accordingly. · Here, "am" (verb) agrees with "I" (subject). · For example: o It is I who am responsible for this mess. (The verb "am" agrees with the subject "I".) · All the other options except (b) are grammatically incorrect. · Therefore, the correct answer is: It is I who am going to be the topper in the upcoming semester. Note: Subjective Pronouns: The pronouns I, you, we, he, she, it, and they are known as subjective pronouns because they act as the subjects of verbs: We went to the party.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Every eligible voter must ____ his/her voter information slip.
questions.explanation: The sentence talks about eligible voters. The blank requires a base verb as it follows the modal verb 'must', which requires a base verb after it. According to this, option (b) is eliminated because it is a third-person singular verb and option (d) is eliminated because it is in past [v2]/past participle [v3] form. The verb 'elope' (run away secretly in order to get married) does not provide coherent meaning to the sentence. So, option (a) is eliminated. The base verb 'check' is the correct choice among the given options. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer. Complete sentence: Every eligible voter must check his/her voter information slip. Note:- Modal verb: A verb used with another verb to express an idea such as possibility that is not expressed by the main verb. The modal verbs in English are "can," "could," "may," "might," "must," "ought," "shall," "should," "will," and "would." They do not change their form (spelling) and they have no infinitive or participle (past/present). Example: I can speak Spanish. Here 'can' expresses the ability of the person to speak (main verb) Spanish. This ability cannot be expressed by the main verb 'speak'. V1 -> Base form/simple form of verb, V2 -> Past form of verb (-ed form or irregular verb), V3 -> Past participle form of verb (-ed, -d, -t, -en, or -n), V4 -> Continuous form of verb / Present participle form of verb (-ing form), V5 -> Third person singular (V1+ -es or -s).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the proverb underlined in this sentence. The ad promised miracles, and the coach said that the proof of the pudding is in the eating, so try it in an actual match.
options: Results are the only reliable test of quality
Advertising claims should be trusted unless disproved
questions.explanation: The proverb "the proof of the pudding is in the eating" means that the true value or quality of something can only be judged by experiencing or testing it, not by promises or appearances. In the sentence, the coach emphasized testing the product in an actual match to see if it really works. So, the correct answer is: (c) Results are the only reliable test of quality

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. MAUDLIN
questions.explanation: The correct answer is (c) Sentimental. MAUDLIN: This means self-pityingly or tearfully sentimental, often in a foolish or exaggerated way. (a) Stoic: This means enduring pain or hardship without showing feelings. This is an antonym. (b) Jocular: This means humorous or playful. This is unrelated. (c) Sentimental: This means prompted by feelings of tenderness, sadness, or nostalgia. Maudlin is an excessive form of this, making it the most appropriate synonym. (d) Pragmatic: This means dealing with things in a sensible and realistic way. This is an antonym.
